title: Projectiles, Edge Triggers, Cooldowns, Lifetimes, and Reuse · Language Clinic: Projectile Spawn
summary: Held fire spawns unbounded bullets, cooldown uses frame count, expired bullets remain in collision groups, pool reuse keeps old velocity, and rapid restart leaks projectiles. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the projectile spawn increment in bounded projectile lifecycle. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Derive muzzle position and velocity from ship transform while deciding whether ship momentum contributes.
Gate shots using simulation time, inclusive boundary tests, pause semantics, and a visible or audible readiness cue.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-projectile-spawn — Derive muzzle position and velocity from ship transform while deciding whether ship momentum contributes.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-projectiles-cooldowns-pools-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-projectiles-cooldowns-pools-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_projectiles_cooldowns_pools_3(requested_shots: int = 12, accepted_shots: int = 5, cooldown_ms: int = 180, lifetime_ms: int = 900, live_cap: int = 6, leaked_projectiles: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if requested_shots < 0 or accepted_shots < 0 or cooldown_ms < 1:
        return False, accepted_shots, "projectile-domain"
    if accepted_shots > requested_shots or accepted_shots > live_cap:
        return False, accepted_shots, "fire-budget"
    if lifetime_ms <= cooldown_ms:
        return False, lifetime_ms, "lifetime-policy"
    if leaked_projectiles > 0:
        return False, leaked_projectiles, "ownership-leak"
    assert accepted_shots <= live_cap
    return True, accepted_shots, "bounded-projectiles"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Projectile Spawn evidence · Theory Projectile Spawn
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-projectile-spawn-1se7rl" that uses case 7603 to gate edge-triggered firing on cooldown, lifetime, live-count, reuse, and deterministic projectile ownership. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Projectile Spawn evidence · Theory Projectile Spawn.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Projectile Spawn into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Projectile Spawn evidence · Theory Projectile Spawn, keep Projectile Spawn connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-projectile-spawn-1se7rl
# Competency: asteroids-projectile-spawn.
# Case 7603: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_projectile_spawn_1se7rl(requested_shots: int = 12, accepted_shots: int = 5, cooldown_ms: int = 180, lifetime_ms: int = 900, live_cap: int = 6, leaked_projectiles: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1se7rl = "1se7rl-lr7es1-7603"
    assert evidence_variant_1se7rl
    evidence_axes_1se7rl = (
        "player1se7rl",
        "seed7603",
        "inputlr7es1",
        "frame1se7rl7603",
        "state76031se7rl",
        "playtestlr7es17603",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1se7rl)) == 6
    if requested_shots < 0 or accepted_shots < 0 or cooldown_ms < 1:
        return False, accepted_shots, "projectile-domain"
    if accepted_shots > requested_shots or accepted_shots > live_cap:
        return False, accepted_shots, "fire-budget"
    if lifetime_ms <= cooldown_ms:
        return False, lifetime_ms, "lifetime-policy"
    if leaked_projectiles > 0:
        return False, leaked_projectiles, "ownership-leak"
    assert accepted_shots <= live_cap
    return True, accepted_shots, "bounded-projectiles"

content.text: The Projectile Spawn structural check is only one gate. bounded projectile lifecycle must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Projectile Spawn: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 7078 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate edge-triggered firing on cooldown, lifetime, live-count, reuse, and deterministic projectile ownership; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive muzzle position and velocity from ship transform while deciding whether ship momentum contributes.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive muzzle position and velocity from ship transform while deciding whether ship momentum contributes.
